sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: TAL Education soars 9.5% after strong earnings beat By Investing.com
feed.summary: TAL Education Group (NYSE:TAL) reported strong third-quarter fiscal 2026 results, with adjusted earnings per share significantly beating analyst expectations at $0.25 versus $0.07. Despite a slight revenue miss, the company saw substantial year-over-year revenue growth of 27% to $770.2 million and a notable improvement in profitability. This positive performance led to a 9.5% jump in the company's shares in pre-market trading.

feed.title: Retirement Systems of Alabama Lowers Stake in Electronic Arts Inc. $EA
feed.summary: Retirement Systems of Alabama significantly reduced its stake in Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) by 91.8% in the third quarter of 2025, selling over 538,000 shares. This move comes as Electronic Arts reported Q3 earnings that missed analysts' estimates, with both EPS and revenue falling short of expectations and revenue declining year-over-year. Despite the stock trading near its 1-year high, analysts currently have a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target below the current trading price.

feed.title: Dow to Cut About 4,500 Jobs, Using AI to Boost Operations
feed.summary: Dow Inc. announced plans to cut approximately 4,500 jobs and utilize AI and automation to streamline operations. This strategic move is projected to deliver at least a $2 billion increase in near-term operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, with a target of $500 million for the current year. The job cuts are part of a broader effort to simplify company processes and boost efficiency.

feed.title: 138,940 Shares in Fortinet, Inc. $FTNT Bought by Retirement Systems of Alabama
feed.summary: The Retirement Systems of Alabama acquired 138,940 shares of Fortinet, Inc. (NASDAQ:FTNT) during the third quarter, a new position valued at approximately $11.68 million. This comes as Fortinet reported strong earnings, exceeding analyst expectations with $0.74 EPS and $1.72 billion in revenue, and provided optimistic FY2025 guidance. Despite insider stock sales by CEO Ken Xie and VP Michael Xie, institutional investors collectively own 83.71% of the company, and analysts generally maintain a "Hold" rating with an average target price of $90.04.

feed.title: ROYAL CARIBBEAN GROUP EXPANDS VACATION PORTFOLIO WITH NEW DISCOVERY CLASS TO BE BUILT AT THE CHANTIERS DE L'ATLANTIQUE SHIPYARD IN FRANCE
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has announced agreements with Chantiers de l'Atlantique for the construction of its new Discovery Class ships, with the first debut anticipated in 2029 and a second in 2032. This partnership continues a long-standing collaboration dating back to 1985 and aims to redefine the vacation experience through innovative design and technology. The new class will offer extraordinary experiences and builds on Royal Caribbean Group's commitment to expanding its global vacation offerings across ocean, river, and land.

feed.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill, Inc. $CMG Shares Sold by Retirement Systems of Alabama

Despite this sell-off, other institutional investors have increased their positions, with institutional ownership now at 91.30%. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for CMG, with a consensus price target of $50.23, while the stock currently trades near $39.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial Inc: Hidden-Player Stock That Might Be Sneakier Than Your Favorite FinTok Guru
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial Inc (AMP) is presented as a "hidden-player" stock that quietly performs while other investors chase meme stocks. The article evaluates AMP as an old-school wealth and financial planning brand, highlighting its stable business model based on recurring revenue from financial advice, wealth management, and insurance. It suggests that Ameriprise, while not flashy or "viral", offers a solid, long-term investment opportunity for those seeking stability and dividend-paying potential over speculative growth.

feed.title: Mutual of America Capital Management LLC Sells 8,213 Shares of D.R. Horton, Inc. $DHI
feed.summary: Mutual of America Capital Management LLC has reduced its stake in D.R. Horton, Inc. (NYSE:DHI) by 19.8%, selling 8,213 shares and now owning 33,346 shares valued at $5.65 million. This follows D.R. Horton exceeding its latest quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, reporting $2.03 EPS and $6.89 billion in revenue despite a 9.5% year-over-year decline in revenue. The company also announced a $0.45 quarterly dividend, and analysts currently have a "Hold" consensus rating with a target price of $160.50.

feed.title: Prediction: 2026 Will Be the Year of PayPal
feed.summary: Despite a recent stock decline, PayPal is positioned for a strong rebound in 2026 due to its undervalued valuation and strategic partnership with OpenAI. The company continues to show revenue and earnings growth, alongside aggressive stock buybacks, indicating a potential for significant returns. The integration of PayPal's payment technology and merchant network with ChatGPT's commerce capabilities could serve as a major catalyst for investor recognition and stock appreciation.

feed.title: META Platforms (NASDAQ:META) Passes Key Peter Lynch Investment Filters
feed.summary: META Platforms (NASDAQ:META) has successfully passed key investment filters derived from Peter Lynch's investment philosophy, known as Growth at a Reasonable Price (GARP). The company demonstrates strong profit expansion, a sensible PEG ratio of 0.91, high return on equity, and robust financial strength with a low debt-to-equity ratio. These factors, combined with Meta's widely used "Family of Apps," suggest it aligns well with Lynch's criteria for a sound investment.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson stock price target raised to $225 from $188 at BofA
feed.summary: BofA Securities has raised its price target for C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) to $225 from $188, maintaining a Buy rating. This upgrade follows the logistics company's strong fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings per share of $1.23, which surpassed analyst expectations, driven by market share gains, AI-driven productivity, and headcount reduction. Despite its current high P/E ratio, InvestingPro data suggests the company's PEG ratio indicates it may be undervalued relative to near-term earnings growth.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Lowers Price Target on SailPoint (SAIL) to $21
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has lowered its price target for SailPoint (SAIL) from $25 to $21, while maintaining a "Neutral" rating. This adjustment reflects a 16% decrease in the price target for the cybersecurity company. Despite this, other analysts have recently reiterated "Buy" or "Outperform" ratings with varying price targets, and the average analyst forecast still suggests a 45.28% upside from the current price.

feed.title: Meet the Future of Autonomous Wildfire Response: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams Advancing in $11M Competition
feed.summary: XPRIZE Wildfire, in partnership with PG&E and the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation, announced five finalist teams advancing in its $11 million competition for autonomous wildfire response. These teams are developing AI and drone-based solutions to detect and suppress high-risk fires rapidly, aiming to transform wildfire management. The finalists received a share of a $750,000 prize and will participate in a final testing round in Alaska in summer 2026.

feed.title: Nashville flyers mourn end of Southwest Airlines' open seating era
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines has ended its long-standing open seating policy in favor of assigned seating, a move that began on January 27th across its network, including Nashville International Airport. This change, which allows customers to pay for specific seats or be assigned one at check-in, follows customer feedback requesting more choices and control. Many travelers at Nashville are mourning the loss of the flexible open seating system, which was a defining feature of Southwest for decades.

feed.title: Dow targets $2 billion EBITDA boost through transformation plan
feed.summary: Dow Inc. has unveiled a "Transform to Outperform" plan aiming for a $2 billion boost in near-term operating EBITDA by simplifying operations and streamlining processes. The initiative involves cutting approximately 4,500 jobs and leveraging AI and automation, with projected EBITDA benefits of $500 million in 2026, $1.2 billion in 2027, and $300 million in 2028. This restructuring addresses profitability challenges and a significant debt burden, complementing a previously announced $1 billion cost savings program.

Despite positive sentiment regarding revenue growth and potential margin recovery from price actions, negative sentiment around the earnings and revenue miss, combined with lower-than-expected Q1 2026 EPS guidance, has pressured the stock.

feed.title: GM Expects To Build More Vehicles In The U.S. Than Ford
feed.summary: General Motors anticipates building more vehicles in the U.S. than Ford Motor Company, a significant shift in domestic production. GM CEO Mary Barra projected an industry-leading 2 million units annually in the coming years due to increased onshoring and strong customer demand. This projection is aided by Ford's temporary production dip as it retools a major plant for EV production, while GM continues to add U.S. production of high-demand internal combustion models.

feed.title: McDonald’s in Mansfield now open after complete rebuild
feed.summary: The McDonald's restaurant in Mansfield, Connecticut, has reopened after a complete rebuild focused on improving parking and traffic flow, especially for drive-through customers. The renovation project, which began last July, aimed to enhance the guest experience and vehicle circulation, reducing indoor seating in favor of a better drive-through experience. The redesign also includes new landscaping, adjustments to parking with EV charging spaces, and a contemporary visual appeal as part of McDonald's evolving brand image.

feed.title: Why California’s fight over ticket fraud has become a proxy war against Ticketmaster and Live Nation
feed.summary: California's proposed AB 1349 bill, aiming to ban speculative ticketing and increase penalties for fraud, has sparked a debate, with critics arguing it disproportionately benefits Ticketmaster and Live Nation by stifling secondary market competition. The bill seeks to protect consumers from fraudulent ticket sales like those experienced by Colorado firefighters Rick Balentine and Tim Cottrell, who were denied entry to an Eagles concert despite purchasing tickets on StubHub. Opponents of the bill contend that defining tickets as licenses and restricting resale options ultimately enhances Ticketmaster's market dominance, forcing fans back to the primary seller and its associated fees.

feed.title: EPAM Sets Earnings Call as Investors Await Full-Year Results
feed.summary: EPAM Systems (NYSE: EPAM) will hold a conference call on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET to discuss its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results. The company, a global provider of digital engineering, cloud services, and AI-enabled transformation, will release its earnings report earlier that morning, and investors and analysts can access the live webcast or a replay through its Investor Relations website. This call is anticipated to shed light on EPAM's performance in 2025 and its future outlook, driven by its expanding AI and digital transformation offerings.

feed.title: Dow reports fourth quarter 2025 results
feed.summary: Dow Inc. reported a challenging fourth quarter and full year 2025, with net sales and operating EBIT significantly down compared to the previous year. Despite a GAAP net loss of $1.5 billion for the quarter and $2.4 billion for the full year, the company emphasized progress on its "Transform to Outperform" program, aiming for $2 billion in additional near-term earnings through cost savings and operational simplification. Financial highlights included a 9% year-over-year decline in net sales to $9.5 billion in Q4 2025 and a full-year net sales totaling $40.0 billion.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE: TMO) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, with revenue growth of 7% and 4% respectively, and significant increases in diluted EPS. The company highlighted strategic advancements including new product launches, expansion of commercial partnerships, and successful capital deployment through M&A and shareholder returns. Thermo Fisher enters 2026 with a strong strategic position, aiming to deliver on its objectives and continue creating value.

feed.title: West to Host Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Conference Call
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. (NYSE: WST) announced it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 12, 2026, before the market opens. The company will follow with a conference call at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results and business expectations. Interested participants can register online to access the live webcast and ask questions.

feed.title: A once-unthinkable C-suite appointment solidifies Walmart’s new identity as a tech company
feed.summary: Walmart's latest C-suite changes, particularly the appointment of David Guggina as CEO of Walmart U.S., signal the retailer's firm shift towards becoming a tech-first company. Guggina, who lacks traditional retail store experience but possesses strong e-commerce, automation, and supply chain expertise, embodies Walmart's strategic focus on technology and AI. This transformation is further underscored by the company's move to Nasdaq and its significant investments in AI-assisted shopping and advertising ventures, contributing to substantial stock growth.

feed.title: AMC+ privacy violations $8.3M class action lawsuit settlement
feed.summary: AMC Networks has agreed to an $8.3 million settlement to resolve a class action lawsuit alleging privacy violations under the federal Video Privacy Protection Act (VPPA). The lawsuit claimed AMC used Meta Pixel tracking to share user activity from services like AMC+, Shudder, and Acorn TV with third parties without consent. Eligible class members who watched videos on AMC's platforms between January 18, 2021, and January 10, 2024, could receive an equal share of the net settlement fund and a one-week digital subscription to AMC+.

feed.title: How Ishares Msci Kokusai Etf (TOK) Affects Rotational Strategy Timing
feed.summary: This article analyzes Ishares MSCI Kokusai ETF (TOK) through an AI-driven lens, suggesting a near-term strong sentiment influencing mid-term neutrality and a long-term positive bias. It provides specific institutional trading strategies (Position, Momentum Breakout, Risk Hedging) with entry points, targets, and stop losses. The analysis also includes multi-timeframe signal analysis, support and resistance levels, and highlights an exceptional 9.8:1 risk-reward setup.

feed.title: Do Wall Street analysts have a positive view of Keurig Dr Pepper shares?
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. (KDP) has seen its stock decline over the past year despite recent strong Q3 2025 earnings which boosted sales and adjusted EPS. While analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus, with an average price target of $34.47 suggesting a 29.1% upside, this sentiment is slightly less optimistic than three months prior. The company's performance has lagged both the S&P 500 and the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ETF.

feed.title: Dow launches Transform to Outperform to raise the competitive industry benchmark for productivity and growth to enable improved returns
feed.summary: Dow (NYSE: DOW) has announced "Transform to Outperform," a comprehensive plan to simplify operations, streamline processes, reset cost structure, and modernize customer service. The initiative targets at least $2 billion in near-term Op. EBITDA improvement by utilizing AI and automation for growth and productivity, aiming to enhance shareholder returns. The plan involves estimated one-time costs of $1.1-1.5 billion, including $600-800 million for severance for approximately 4,500 roles.

feed.title: SLB Records Major Loss Linked To Carbon Capture Project
feed.summary: SLB, a global energy services company, has reported a significant financial loss of approximately $210 million related to an impairment within its carbon capture joint venture, SLB Capturi, highlighting the commercial risks of large-scale carbon capture deployment. Despite this setback, SLB Capturi continues to expand its project portfolio in Europe, with operational projects in Denmark, Norway, and the Netherlands. The situation illustrates the dual reality of rapid growth in announced carbon capture projects alongside ongoing challenges in cost control and financial viability within the sector.

feed.title: Bain, Blackstone Among Suitors for UK Vitamin Maker Vitabiotics
feed.summary: Bain Capital and Blackstone Inc. are reportedly among the private equity firms advancing to the next round of bidding for Vitabiotics Ltd., a UK-based vitamin company. Other firms like EQT AB and TPG Inc. are also in the running for the business, which could be valued at approximately £900 million ($1.2 billion). Vitabiotics, known for popular supplement brands in the UK, is led by former Dragons’ Den investor Tej Lalvani.
